seoTitle: How to Undo a Bank Reconciliation in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to undo a previous bank reconciliation in QuickBooks Online so you can fix errors and get your records back to an accurate state.
intro: Sometimes a reconciliation gets completed with an error buried inside, and the fix is to undo it and start over. This guide explains how to reverse a previous bank reconciliation in QuickBooks Online without losing your transaction history.

steps.heading: Go to the reconciliation history
steps.body: Navigate to the reconcile section and find the history of past reconciliations for the account in question.

steps.heading: Select the reconciliation to undo
steps.body: Find the specific reconciliation period that contains the error you need to correct.

steps.heading: Undo the reconciliation
steps.body: Use the undo option for that period, which will reopen the transactions as unreconciled.

steps.heading: Fix the error and reconcile again
steps.body: Correct the transaction that caused the issue, then rerun the reconciliation for that period.

faq.question: Will undoing a reconciliation delete any transactions?
faq.answer: No, undoing a reconciliation only changes the reconciled status of transactions, it doesn't delete them.

faq.question: Can I undo a reconciliation from several months ago?
faq.answer: Yes, but undoing older reconciliations can affect subsequent periods, so proceed carefully and review what follows.

faq.question: Do I need accountant access to undo a reconciliation?
faq.answer: Certain undo options may require accountant-level access, depending on how far back the reconciliation is.

faq.question: What should I do after undoing a reconciliation?
faq.answer: Identify and fix the transaction causing the discrepancy, then redo the reconciliation to confirm it matches your bank statement.
